article_title: Forgejo 15.0 Adds Repository-Scoped Tokens, OIDC Support, and Ephemeral Runners in New LTS Release
summary: Submission needs source-list fixes before publication

findings.message: Body cites a factual source URL that is missing from the submission's sources array
findings.details: The article uses https://forgejo.org/docs/latest/admin/release-schedule/ to support the LTS/support-window claims in the Overview, What We Know, and Analysis sections, but that URL is not present in article.sources. As submitted, the provenance/source snapshot does not include every source the article relies on.

content_preview.title: Forgejo 15.0 Adds Repository-Scoped Tokens, OIDC Support, and Ephemeral Runners in New LTS Release
content_preview.summary: Forgejo 15.0 lands as the project’s new LTS release with repository-scoped access tokens, reusable workflow expansion, OpenID Connect support, and ephemeral runners.
content_preview.body_excerpt:
## Overview

Forgejo v15.0 shipped on April 16, 2026 as the project's 100th release and its new long-term-support branch, according to the [official Forgejo announcement](https://forgejo.org/2026-04-release-v15-0/), [LWN](https://lwn.net/Articles/1068001/), and the [Forgejo release schedule](https://forgejo.org/docs/latest/admin/release-schedule/). The release keeps pushing the self-hosted Git forge toward more opinionated security and automation defaults, with repository-scoped access tokens, e...

recommendations: Consider adding trusted domains to config/source_allowlist.txt

editor_notes.content_quality: The article is well-structured and the core release details are clearly explained for a developer audience.
editor_notes.source_verification: Fetched and read all 3 listed sources plus the additional Forgejo release-schedule page cited inline in the body. The official Forgejo release announcement supports the release date, repository-scoped tokens, reusable workflow expansion, OIDC support, ephemeral runners, UI changes, and breaking-change notes. LWN confirms the release and the LTS/support dates. Linuxiac supports the OIDC, ephemeral-runner, setup, and breaking-change summaries. The Forgejo release-schedule page supports the LTS/support-window claims, but it is cited in the article without being included in article.sources.
editor_notes.factual_accuracy: The factual claims checked out against the fetched sources, but the submission does not fully enumerate all sources it relies on. That is a provenance and source-accounting defect that must be corrected before approval.
editor_notes.originality: No duplicate Forgejo 15.0 coverage found in the archive.
concerns: The submission's sources array is incomplete because it omits the Forgejo release-schedule URL used for factual claims.
recommendations: Add https://forgejo.org/docs/latest/admin/release-schedule/ to article.sources and regenerate the signed submission.
Alternatively, remove or rewrite any claims that depend on that URL so every cited factual source is represented in article.sources.
editor_notes.overall_assessment: Promising submission, but it should not be published until the source list fully matches the URLs used to support factual claims in the body.
